title: Cordyloblaste confusa
taxonomy: Cordyloblaste confusa is a species of coniferous tree that belongs to the family Podocarpaceae. It is native to Taiwan and is a member of the genus Cordyloblaste. The species is closely related to the genus Afrocarpus and is often classified as a member of the Afrocarpus-Cordyloblaste clade. This classification is based on molecular phylogenetic studies that have helped to resolve the relationships among these coniferous tree species. Further research may provide more information about the classification and evolutionary history of Cordyloblaste confusa.

intro: Cordyloblaste confusa, also known as the Japanese silver fir, is a species of coniferous tree endemic to Taiwan. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as the potential to boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Cordyloblaste confusa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cordyloblaste confusa is commonly used to prepare tea and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
